name: Richard Boykin
bio: Richard Boykin is listed as a Democratic candidate in the 2026 IL-07 open-seat primary. He previously served on the Cook County Board of Commissioners. (Source: Shaw Local candidate list, March 5, 2026: https://www.shawlocal.com/northwest-herald/2026/03/05/illinois-primary-ballot-heres-whos-running-in-the-march-17-election/) Boykin served as chief of staff to Rep. Danny K. Davis (1997–2006) before winning Cook County Commissioner District 1 in 2014. He lost reelection in 2018 to Brandon Johnson's predecessor. He received a J.D. from the University of Dayton and a B.A. from Central State University. He ran for Cook County Board President in 2018, losing to Toni Preckwinkle. Currently lives in Oak Park, IL.

jacket.note: ~$383K raised as of Feb 25, 2026 (FEC). FEC ID: H6IL07370
jacket.donors_note: No itemized donor breakdown available yet. total_raised reflects FEC aggregate total (H6IL07370) — detailed donor records pending ETL import.

key_votes.bill: Cook County Sweetened Beverage Tax ("Soda Tax")
key_votes.summary: Boykin was the first Democrat to oppose the Cook County sweetened beverage tax in 2016, then led the Board to repeal it in 2017 over President Toni Preckwinkle's objections.
Boykin led the initiative to place a county-wide referendum on expanded mental health treatment funding on the November 2014 ballot. The referendum passed with over 1.4 million votes.

career_history.role: Candidate for Cook County Board President
career_history.highlight: Ran against Toni Preckwinkle; lost in primary
